organizations.role: Public supporter / donor (self-stated pro-Israel political interest)
organizations.notes: Loewy Law Firm leadership bio and Texas Hillel alumni spotlight both state AIPAC is among organizations the Loewys actively support. Adam has "keen interest in pro-Israel politics and is involved with AIPAC." Disambiguation: Velasquez batch Loewy, Adam / law loewy / Austin 78731 = Adam Jacob Loewy, Loewy Law Firm P.C.
Loewy Law Firm bio lists AIPAC among organizations Phil and Adam actively support.

organizations.role: Former AIPAC activist (Stanford Law early 1990s, historical)
organizations.notes: Self-disclosed former AIPAC activist during Stanford Law years. Has since shifted to two-state solution advocacy. Historical AIPAC tie only — not current supporter.
With husband Kirk Rudy (Endeavor co-founder, ADL board)
NATIONAL board (not just Austin chapter). Lubetzky Family Foundation Frontline Impact Project mobilized aid to Israel after Oct 7 2023.

organizations.org: ADL Workplace Pledge to Fight Antisemitism

organizations.notes: Multiple independent sources confirm ADL board role. Also on Science Mill board, NWACA, etc.

Sandy (Sanford) Dochen, longtime Austin civic leader; UT Austin grad, 21-year IBM career as corporate social responsibility/citizenship director (retired 2019), now affiliated with Amplify Credit Union. 78731 (Northwest Hills) matches. Sits on numerous boards. Gave to both Brigid Shea and Ann Howard.
Founded 2002 to promote Israeli-Palestinian two-state solution. Liberal Zionist peace org.
Israeli-Palestinian peace org from which OneVoice emerged

organizations.org: Austin Women in Oil and Gas
organizations.role: Administrative Chair and Board of Directors (2019-present)
organizations.notes: Partner at Holland & Knight (formerly Thompson & Knight); practice covers oil & gas regulatory and transactional matters in Eagle Ford, Bakken, Permian Basin

organizations.org: Kay Bailey Hutchison Center for Energy, Law & Business (UT Austin)
organizations.notes: Energy law and business advisory council at UT Austin
